commentary: Green bonds are positioned as the most scalable fixed-income instrument to capture Europe's energy-transition financing requirement of ~€660bn per year to 2030. The EU Green Bond Standard reduces greenwashing risk through taxonomy alignment, mandatory disclosure, and external verification. GSAM research shows diversification benefits with minimal impact on duration or yield, making green bonds a structural allocation rather than a satellite sleeve.

GSAM presents China as dominant in resource supply chains but offset by structural cracks and external pressure, netting to a neutral country-market read. Evidence: China's current position in supply-chain control is unmatched. It is dominant in rare earth mining and refining—inputs critical to EVs, wind, defense, and AI. ... Structural cracks remain. An aging population and slowing growth squeeze fiscal room. Property-sector stress drags on demand. US-led tech restrictions are forcing costly chip import-substitution. And overcapacity in solar, batteries, and EVs, while an export advantage, is fueling trade friction with the EU and US.. Locator: p.5. Basis: inferred — single-step analyst inference from macro/thematic prose; segregated one band below stated calls, review required.

GSAM keeps a medium-term equity overweight but pairs it with a short-term neutral stance, so the horizon-mixed equity view nets to neutral. Evidence: overweight equities medium-term but neutral short-term. Locator: p.6. Checker: unconfirmed (Medium-term overweight paired with short-term neutral arguably nets to O rather than N.).
